items.title: USDA approves Arkansas SNAP waiver restricting certain drinks and candy starting July 1, 2026
items.summary: USDA’s Food and Nutrition Service approved Arkansas’ request to modify the restricted food and beverage definitions under its SNAP food restriction waiver on May 12, 2026. The waiver is approved for two years, effective July 1, 2026, and the June 10, 2025 approval letter lists excluded items including soda (including low/no-calorie), fruit/vegetable drinks with less than 50% natural juice, other unhealthy drinks, and candy.

items.title: USDA sets FY 2026 WIC fruit-and-vegetable cash-value benefit amounts (children $26; pregnant/postpartum $48; breastfeeding $52)
items.summary: USDA WIC Policy Memorandum #2026-2 (dated Dec. 10, 2025) lists the FY 2026 monthly cash-value voucher/benefit (CVV/B) amounts for fruits and vegetables as $26 for children, $48 for pregnant and postpartum participants, and $52 for fully or mostly breastfeeding participants. The memo cites an FY 2026 inflation adjustment of 11.8% over FY 2022 baseline amounts.

items.title: HUD says June Housing Choice Voucher (Section 8) HAP deposits were delayed one day and sent June 2, 2026
items.summary: HUD told public housing agencies that there was a one-day delay in the deposit of June Housing Choice Voucher (HCV) Housing Assistance Payments (HAP), and that payments would be deposited the next day (June 2, 2026). HUD said it worked with the U.S. Treasury to address the issue and that it had been resolved.

items.title: USDA posts FY 2026 SNAP income limits window and maximum monthly benefit amounts (contiguous U.S./DC)
items.summary: USDA’s SNAP eligibility page notes that the published income eligibility limits apply to applications between Oct. 1, 2025 and Sept. 30, 2026. For households in the 48 contiguous states and the District of Columbia, the maximum monthly allotments listed are $298 (1 person), $546 (2), $785 (3), and $994 (4).

items.title: IRS reminder: Child Tax Credit/ACTC requires an SSN valid for work and issued before the 2025 return due date
items.summary: The IRS EITC Central “Tax preparer toolkit” states that for tax year 2025, to qualify for the Child Tax Credit and Additional Child Tax Credit, the taxpayer (or spouse if filing jointly) and each qualifying child must have a Social Security number valid for employment in the U.S. and issued before the due date of the 2025 return. The page also notes that if a taxpayer doesn’t meet the SSN requirement, they aren’t eligible for the credit and do not need to respond to the notice.

items.title: CMS: Some clinical labs must report private payer data May 1–July 31, 2026 (Medicare CLFS reporting)
items.summary: CMS’ MLN Connects newsletter (June 4, 2026) highlights Clinical Diagnostic Laboratory Reporting and states the reporting period runs from May 1 through July 31, 2026 for applicable laboratories. CMS also lists other near-term effective dates in the same newsletter, including updates beginning July 1, 2026 and October 1, 2026 for certain Medicare billing/coding items.

items.title: USDA issues memo transmitting 2026–2027 WIC income eligibility guidelines
items.summary: USDA’s FNS documents listing shows a policy memorandum titled “Publication of 2026-2027 WIC Income Eligibility Guidelines” dated April 29, 2026. The memo transmits the 2026–2027 income eligibility guidelines (IEGs) used by WIC agencies when determining whether applicants meet income requirements.
